query: North Korea nuclear test 2026 - any signs of imminent 7th nuclear test, activity at Punggye-ri test site, recent statements or preparations mid-2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on a potential 7th North Korean nuclear test as of July 2026:

## Summary

**Bottom line:** No 7th nuclear test has occurred (last was September 2017), and there are **no publicly reported signs of an *imminent* test**. The site is technically ready and has been for years — the only remaining trigger is Kim Jong Un's political decision.

**Punggye-ri site status:**
- **Tunnel No. 3** has been assessed as test-ready since ~mid-2022 (cabling, instrumentation, drainage in place)
- 2024 satellite activity was assessed by CSIS as **routine caretaker maintenance**, not imminent-test indicators
- Tunnels 1 & 2 collapsed; Tunnel 4 saw intermittent work (possible expansion or deception)
- September 2024 flooding damaged access bridges; repairs followed

**Key intelligence:**
- **South Korea's DIA (Nov 5, 2025):** North Korea "technically ready" to detonate at Tunnel No. 3 "on short notice," pending only Kim's decision
- **U.S. DIA (2025):** Site "fully restored," capable at North Korea's discretion, no confirmatory activity

**Mid-2026 trajectory:** North Korea's demonstrated focus has been on **expanding fissile-material production** rather than testing. In June 2026, Kim inspected a new uranium enrichment plant and pledged to grow nuclear forces "at an exponential rate" (claiming production capacity more than doubled in 5 years). In early July 2026, he observed nuclear-capable cruise missile tests aboard a new destroyer — delivery-system activity, not a detonation.

The pattern points to industrializing warhead production rather than moving toward a demonstrative test, though analysts consistently caution there may be **few observable warning indicators** before a test since preparations are already complete.

Note: AskNews tools hit credit/tier limits, so I relied on web search, Exa, and direct source fetches (NPR, CNN, Army Recognition, CSIS Beyond Parallel, 38 North). Full sources and data points are logged in the structured output.

sources.snippet: Nuclear tests conducted October 2006, May 2009, February 2013, January 2016, September 2016, and September 2017.

sources.snippet: Since Dec 2021, reactivation of parts of site, especially South Portal (Tunnel No. 3) restoration.

key_facts: No 7th North Korean nuclear test has occurred as of July 2026; the last (sixth) test was September 3, 2017.
There are no publicly reported signs of an imminent test as of mid-2026; readiness has existed for years and the decision rests solely with Kim Jong Un.

South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency (Nov 5, 2025 closed-door briefing) assessed North Korea is technically ready to detonate at Punggye-ri Tunnel No. 3 on short notice, pending only Kim's political decision.
A 2025 U.S. Defense Intelligence Agency assessment judged Punggye-ri fully restored and capable of a 7th test at North Korea's discretion, with no confirmatory test activity verified.

Tunnel No. 3 (South Portal) has been assessed as ready to field a test since roughly mid-2022; Tunnels 1 and 2 are collapsed/abandoned; Tunnel 4 saw intermittent work interpreted as expansion or deception.
CSIS Beyond Parallel characterized 2024 site activity (snow clearing, personnel, vehicle tracks) as routine caretaker maintenance to preserve readiness, not signs of imminent testing.
September 2024 monsoon flooding washed out at least seven vehicle bridges near Punggye-ri portals; October 2024 imagery showed road repairs.

On June 3, 2026, Kim Jong Un inspected a new uranium enrichment plant (assessed near Yongbyon) and pledged to boost nuclear forces 'at an exponential rate,' claiming fissile-material capacity had more than doubled versus five years earlier.
North Korea's demonstrated mid-2026 priority is expanding fissile material and warhead production, not conducting a nuclear detonation.
In early July 2026, Kim observed nuclear-capable cruise missile tests aboard the new 5,000-ton destroyer Kang Kon — delivery-system activity, not a nuclear test.
Arsenal estimates range from an older 20-60 warheads to some current expert estimates of over 100; prior NIS reporting cited ~70 kg of plutonium plus significant HEU.
On Jan 28, 2026, Kim signaled North Korea would soon unveil 'next-stage' nuclear plans, with a five-year defense/economy plan expected at an upcoming party congress.
